questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} Option (B) is the correct mirror image of the given combination.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} In the following diagram, the circle represents ‘rural folk’, the triangle represents ‘businessmen’ and the rectangle represents ‘graduates’. Study the diagram carefully and answer the question that follows. What is the number of rural businessmen, who are NOT graduates?
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} 8 is the only number which represents rural and businessmen, but not belongs to graduates section.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} Select the option in which the words share the same relationship as that shared by the given pair of words. Food: Cook ::?
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} A person who cooks is called cook. Similarly, A person who makes gold jewelry and other gold items is called Goldsmith.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} Select the option figure in which the given figure is embedded (rotation is NOT allowed).
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} Given figure is embedded in Option (D).

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} img {max-width:100% !important} Select the option in which the numbers are related in the same way as are the numbers of the following set. (16, 7, 37 ) (28, 9, 55 )

img {max-width:100% !important} Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements. Statements: All Good are Bad. Some Best are Good. Conclusions: I. Some Best are Bad. II. All Good are Best.
options: img {max-width:100% !important} Both conclusions I and II follows.
img {max-width:100% !important} Neither conclusion I nor II follows.

img {max-width:100% !important} Pointing to a Gudiya in a cradle, Tanuj said, "She is the daughter of my father's mother's only daughter's elder daughter". How is the Gudiya's mother related to Tanuj ?
img {max-width:100% !important} Select the option that is related to the third word in the same way as the second word is related to the first word. Canada : Ottawa :: Albania : ?
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} Ottawa is the capital of Canada. Similarly, Tirana is the capital of Albania.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} Select the correct option that indicates the arrangement of the given words in the reverse order in which they appear in an English dictionary. 1. Topology 2. Topper 3. Tolerance 4. Together 5. Tomorrow
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} Words are arranged in reverse order as per English dictionary in Option (D).

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} How many triangles are there in the given figure?
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} The total number of triangles in the given figure are 11.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} Four letter-clusters have been given, out of which three are alike in some manner and one is different. Select the letter-cluster that is different.
img {max-width:100% !important} In a certain code language, ‘RAT’ is written as ‘WDU’ and ‘DOG’ is written as ‘JRG’. How will ‘COW’ be written in that language?
img {max-width:100% !important} Four words have been given, out of which three are alike in some manner and one is different. Select the word that is different.
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} Except Option (A) all are the terms related to body. Ecology – the study of organisms and how they interact with the environment around them. Myology - the study of the structure, arrangement, and action of the muscles. Ophthalmology- the study of the eye conditions Dermatology - the study of the skin, hair, and nails.
